patient_001.q: Has this patient had similar symptoms before?
patient_001.a: Yes. The current kidney inflammation connects to a malar rash (Mar 2023), intermittent joint pain (Jan 2024), and an elevated ANA titer (Apr 2024) -- an autoimmune cluster spanning two years.
Fatigue began 3 days after the Metoprolol dose increase to 50mg in Feb 2022. It is most consistent with a medication effect rather than an unexplained cause.

patient_001.q: Are there any dangerous drug interactions right now?
patient_001.a: Yes. Clarithromycin inhibits CYP3A4, which clears atorvastatin. Combined with reduced kidney function (eGFR 58), statin exposure and toxicity risk rise ~62%.

patient_001.q: What explains the morning glucose spikes?
patient_001.a: They follow nights with REM sleep under 45 minutes, which follow late dinners. It is a self-reinforcing loop now tracking with weight gain and prediabetes.

patient_001.q: What is the patient's mental-health pattern?
patient_001.a: Anxiety is periodic: Sunday evenings and the week before quarterly performance reviews. Therapy notes a year later independently confirmed the same pattern.

patient_002.a: Yes. Over 60 days, family-relationship recall dropped ~32% and language complexity fell from 100 to 79. The decline is steady, not episodic.
